summary: ANNEX_AG (all FAP anti-gaming protections) is PROPOSED — AG1 pilot representativeness, AG3 adversarial auditor, AG4 urgency bypass reclassification, AG5 stagnation quality, AG7 concentration controls are all inoperative.
failure_mode: Every founding-window protection can be gamed during the most vulnerable period before the FAP is hardened.
recommended_fix_shape: Activate ANNEX_AG before or concurrent with P-013 ACTIVE; treat AG1 and AG3 as pre-launch gates equivalent to the Ombuds seating gate.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: AG4 urgency bypass prohibition blocks emergency workarounds — requires careful scoping to operational continuity exceptions.

summary: CIP removal requires 6-of-7 Plenum vote on a 5-member body — no acceptance test catches this contradiction; captured CIP member is permanently irremovable.
failure_mode: No acceptance test validates whether downstream rules referencing Plenum size are internally consistent; gate clears without checking impossibility.
recommended_fix_shape: Add a pre-activation consistency check: before ANNEX_AM is activated, the removal threshold must be verifiably achievable on the stated Plenum size.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: Low — additive gate check, no structural change.

category: No recurrence schedule for pre-launch gates
summary: All pre-launch gates are one-time checks with no recurrence schedule; post-launch drift in gated conditions is undetected.
failure_mode: Founding team clears gates legitimately; conditions degrade post-launch; no gate re-triggers; corpus reads as compliant while operational system is non-compliant.
recommended_fix_shape: Add recurrence schedules to the five most critical gates: Ombuds seating (annual), oracle methodology registry (semi-annual), capture dashboard (quarterly), evidence ladder (per status change), implementation drift audit (annual).

summary: Structural vs. procedural objection classification is made by the founding coalition — the entity being evaluated classifies the challenges to its own legitimacy.
failure_mode: Founding coalition classifies all objections as procedural, proceeds at 60-day mark with no extensions.
recommended_fix_shape: Require objection classification to be made by an independent body designated before the window opens.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: Medium — requires identifying an independent classifier before the founding window, which may not exist.

summary: Founding Legitimacy Dossier is self-certified by the founding coalition; no independent verifier is named and no external attestation is required.
failure_mode: Founding coalition prepares and certifies its own legitimacy dossier; gate clears on self-certification.
recommended_fix_shape: Add independent attestation requirement for FLD: conflict register and exit rehearsal findings must be independently certified by the oppose-coalition nominating body or equivalent.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: Medium — requires identifying independent certifier before gate.

summary: No acceptance test detects shared AI training provenance between oracle nodes; FC-032 pairwise correlation does not detect correlated directional bias below the 0.30 threshold.
failure_mode: Multiple oracles share the same proprietary AI system; pairwise correlation is within limit; systematic bias produces simultaneous directional error.
recommended_fix_shape: Add mandatory AI supply chain disclosure to oracle accreditation intake; flag shared upstream AI vendors as a concentration trigger.

summary: IOA trigger standard is undefined; per-domain cap is cyclable; no acceptance test validates whether an IOA invocation was warranted.
failure_mode: Actor cycles through domains, maintaining near-continuous IOA governance; no cap breached; no acceptance test fires.
recommended_fix_shape: Add cumulative cross-domain IOA duration metric to capture dashboard; require supermajority CRP review if any region is under IOA for more than 4 quarters in a rolling 8-quarter window.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: Medium — must not block legitimate emergency response.

summary: Demurrage safe harbor contradiction (6 months vs 18 months) is undetectable by any acceptance test; no gate checks numerical consistency between Constitution and implementing annexes.
failure_mode: Account holder cites ANNEX_J 18-month figure; enforcement authority cites 6-month Constitution; no precedence rule; demurrage practically unenforceable.
recommended_fix_shape: Add corpus consistency check gate: any ACTIVE implementing annex specifying a numerical value also in the Constitution must match or include explicit precedence declaration.

summary: FAP Tier 2 approval has no minimum deliberation time, no written-reasoning requirement, and no minimum evidentiary checklist; AG4 reclassification is PROPOSED and inoperative.
failure_mode: Tier 2 amendment approved in minutes without substantive review.
recommended_fix_shape: Add mandatory minimum deliberation period (7 days Tier 2, 30 days Tier 1-adjacent) and a structured reasoning template whose completion is a gate condition.

summary: No acceptance test monitors whether adversarial oracle seat continues to be independently selected after accreditation; AI supply chain drift after accreditation is undetected.
failure_mode: Oracle node changes its AI system between accreditation cycles; methodology-class registry shows node compliant; systematic bias accumulates undetected.
recommended_fix_shape: Add re-accreditation trigger: any oracle node changing its AI system, data source, or standards-body relationship must re-submit accreditation package.

summary: Evidence ladder gate requires 'failure criteria' for status changes but does not specify they must be falsifiable or measurable — an unfalsifiable criterion clears the gate while preventing accountability.
failure_mode: Status change to ACTIVE with failure criteria expressed only as general goals, not operationally testable thresholds.
recommended_fix_shape: Add falsifiability requirement: failure criteria must specify a measurable threshold, measurement method, and named measurement authority.

summary: Bootstrap circularity determination is made by the bootstrap review itself — the same review that benefits from finding circularity by unlocking the expedited pathway.
failure_mode: Bootstrapping actor classifies own dependency as genuinely circular; expedited pathway activates; standard FAP evidence requirements bypassed.
recommended_fix_shape: Require the circularity determination to be made by the Federated Ombuds duty sub-node, not the bootstrap review; publish finding with 14-day challenge window.

summary: No acceptance test or capture dashboard metric tracks cumulative IOA duration, domain-cycling patterns, or IOA-decision ratio in CRP agenda.
failure_mode: Region normalizes emergency governance through domain cycling; pattern visible only in retrospect; no actor is automatically alerted.
recommended_fix_shape: Capture dashboard baseline gate must include emergency normalization metrics: cumulative emergency-mode duration per region, domain-cycling flags, IOA-to-normal-governance decision ratio.

summary: Founding panel conflict attestations are 'externally verified' but the external verifier is not named and the verification standard is not specified.
failure_mode: Founding coalition retains a nominally external verifier that performs cursory checks; structural dependence is invisible in the activation record.
recommended_fix_shape: Name a verification standard and require the verifier to be nominated by the oppose-coalition nominating body, not selected by the founding coalition.

summary: Buterin penalty for adversarial panel defection triggers on 'improperly admitted' Tier 1 proposals — determination requires 4-of-5 Ombuds Plenum vote, which may arrive long after harm is irreversible.
failure_mode: Adversarial panel member defects; improper admission detected late; Tier 1 amendment in effect months before reversal process begins.
recommended_fix_shape: UNCERTAIN — payoff matrix favors honest behavior ex ante; residual risk acknowledged; consider adding interim suspension of improperly-admitted amendments pending penalty determination.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: Medium — interim suspension could be misused to delay legitimate amendments.

summary: ANNEX_AZ §AZ2 contains demurrage architecture, not the Tier 0 token mechanism specification required by §AK8.1 and INV-LAUNCH-1; the launch gate is permanently unblockable.
failure_mode: INV-LAUNCH-1 cannot be cleared; operational phase with real persons cannot legally proceed.
recommended_fix_shape: Draft new Tier 0 pseudonymous token mechanism specification at ANNEX_AZ §AZ2 or a new annex; update §AK8.1 reference to correct location.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: Low — additive specification; no existing text displaced.

summary: CIP removal requires '6-of-7 vote of the Federated Ombuds Plenum' but the Plenum has 5 members (FC-090); the removal mechanism is structurally inoperative.
failure_mode: Captured CIP member is irremovable; no valid removal vote can ever be cast.
recommended_fix_shape: Fix A: change to 'unanimous vote (5-of-5) of the Federated Ombuds Plenum'. Fix B: change to '4-of-5 Plenum vote' (matching FC-091 supermajority standard). Fix C (expensive): expand Plenum to 7 via Tier 1 amendment.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: Medium — Fix A creates single-veto concern; Fix B reduces protection level; Fix C has catch-22 (needs Tier 1 amendment which captured CIP can block). Human judgment required.

summary: Irremovable captured CIP member can also block all Tier 1 amendments (requires 5 affirmative CIP votes; 3 captured members reduce achievable votes to 4).
failure_mode: Permanent Tier 1 amendment deadlock if 3+ CIP members are captured; system cannot self-correct through normal amendment process.
recommended_fix_shape: Fix SVS-002 first; if recursive lock already triggered, H-3 refounding convention may be required.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: High — recursive lock may require H-3 if already triggered; H-3 is a drastic measure.

summary: All founding-window protections (ANNEX_AG, ANNEX_AF) are PROPOSED during the founding window they are designed to protect.
failure_mode: Urgency bypass, cherry-picked pilots, and grace exploitation are all unguarded during the highest institutional capture risk period.
recommended_fix_shape: Add AG4 and AG1 as mandatory pre-launch blocking gates in Acceptance_Protocol.md; expedite AG4 activation.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: Low — additive gate; no existing architecture changed.

summary: AG4 (urgency bypass prohibition) and AG1 (pilot representativeness) are absent from the mandatory pre-launch blocking gate list.
failure_mode: Founding-window patches can be activated through urgency bypass without evidence; pilot evidence comes from favorable regions.
recommended_fix_shape: Add AG4 and AG1 as pre-launch blocking gates.

summary: No objective standard for Level 3/4 emergency declaration; IOA can be constituted on manufactured declaration with no declaring authority or evidence requirement.
failure_mode: Emergency pretext activation of IOA; above-CSM Essential Access reduction using manufactured supply crisis.
recommended_fix_shape: Define objective trigger criteria for Level 3/4 declaration independent of declaring body's judgment; add oracle confirmation requirement before IOA activation.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: Medium — must not block legitimate emergency response.

summary: Oracle suspension trigger fires but owner, timeline, appeal, and audit artifact are undefined.
failure_mode: Oracle cohort flags correlated nodes; suspension declared but by whom and on what timeline is unknown.
recommended_fix_shape: Designate RCS accreditation body as suspension owner; add 14-day resolution timeline; add Ombuds notification requirement; add accreditation record update as mandatory audit artifact.

summary: AM3 administrative hollowing triggers fire but the initiating body (CIP) may not yet be constituted during the founding window.
failure_mode: Administrative hollowing (unfilled seats, lapsed post-mortems, oracle degradation) is undetected during founding window.
recommended_fix_shape: Designate Federated Ombuds Plenum as AM3 review initiator before CIP constitution; add explicit interim responsibility assignment.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: Low — clarifies existing text; no structural change.

summary: Sub-2% oracle understatement of annual Flow issuance can systematically starve CIP funding below operational floor without triggering the published-discrepancy mechanism.
failure_mode: Slow CIP defunding through oracle understatement; CIP operational capacity degrades over years without any single trigger firing.
recommended_fix_shape: Lower materiality threshold in §AM8.3 from >2% to >0.5% for CIP budget purposes; require quarterly instead of annual comparison.

summary: '(to be drafted)' language in §AK8.1 confirms token mechanism spec was known missing at authoring time; no tracking as a blocking gate.
failure_mode: Known design debt not tracked as a pre-launch blocking gate; launch can proceed with this known gap unless explicitly blocked.
recommended_fix_shape: Add explicit tracking of this as a pre-launch blocking gate; assign authoring responsibility.

section: ANNEX_AK §AK8 (access tiers); ANNEX_AZ §AZ3 (demurrage tiers); Acceptance_Protocol.md (governance tiers)
summary: 'Tier' used in three incompatible senses (governance amendment tier, identity access tier, demurrage rate tier) without namespace disambiguation.
failure_mode: Implementers and auditors misread governance tier references as access or demurrage tier references; cross-document analysis errors.
recommended_fix_shape: Add namespace disambiguation: 'Governance Tier' (1/2/3), 'Access Tier' (0/1), 'Demurrage Tier' (T1/T2/T3/T4); add glossary entry.

summary: 'Human dignity' appears in purpose statements and invariants without a corpus-wide operational definition.
failure_mode: Dignity-based protection claims cannot be operationalized, appealed, or adjudicated without a corpus definition.
recommended_fix_shape: Add 'human dignity' as a P-004 protected term with operational definition drawn from AK7 procedural floor and INV-001 survival guarantee.

summary: ANNEX_AZ §AZ2 contains demurrage definitions, not the Tier 0 token mechanism specification explicitly marked '(to be drafted)' by §AK8.1; INV-LAUNCH-1 pre-operational gate cannot be verified.
failure_mode: INV-LAUNCH-1 pre-operational gate cannot be verified against the referenced section; launch is blocked.
recommended_fix_shape: Draft Tier 0 token mechanism specification at a new location (or add §AZ2-TOKEN subsection to ANNEX_AZ); update all references.

summary: FC-YT1 and FC-YT2 do not appear as registered rows in the founding/commitments.md commitment table; they exist as provisional values in ANNEX_Y text only.
failure_mode: INV-LAUNCH-1 gate condition (a) is unmet — values are not in the canonical binding location.
recommended_fix_shape: Register FC-YT1 and FC-YT2 as formal rows in founding/commitments.md with binding notation.

summary: Federated Ombuds Plenum not yet constituted; INV-LAUNCH-1 requires Ombuds verification as a structural co-condition; gate cannot be cleared.
failure_mode: INV-LAUNCH-1 gate condition (b) cannot be cleared until Ombuds is seated.
recommended_fix_shape: Pre-launch gate: constitute Federated Ombuds (4-of-5 seats) before any enrollment.

summary: CIP removal requires '6-of-7 vote of the Federated Ombuds Plenum' but the Plenum has 5 members; removal mechanism is structurally inoperative.
failure_mode: CIP members cannot be removed; misconduct cannot be sanctioned.
recommended_fix_shape: Amend AM8.4 to read '4-of-5 vote of the Federated Ombuds Plenum' (matching FC-091); or clarify that removal requires 6-of-7 CIP members (a different body — HUMAN JUDGMENT REQUIRED).
risk_introduced_by_fixing: High — all fix options have structural problems; requires human judgment on which body was intended.

summary: CIP concurrent ratification requirement for Tier 1 amendments is not stated in the Constitution or amendment_protocol.md; only appears in ANNEX_AM §AM8.6.
failure_mode: Tier 1 amendment completed without CIP ratification; validity of amendment disputed retroactively.
recommended_fix_shape: Add explicit reference to CIP concurrent ratification in amendment_protocol.md §3.

summary: Demurrage safe harbor: 6 months (Constitution) vs. 18 months (ANNEX_J); ANNEX_J has detailed rationale for 18 months but no governing clause making it controlling over the Constitution.
failure_mode: Enforcement against 6-month figure; 12 additional months of large-balance savings exposed to demurrage contrary to ANNEX_J design intent.
recommended_fix_shape: Add governing clause to ANNEX_J: 'Where the Humane Constitution §VIII states 6 months, this annex controls and the 18-month figure governs.'
risk_introduced_by_fixing: Medium — human judgment required: which figure is correct?

summary: No public notice is required before Tier 1 amendment signatures are collected; 180-day timelock begins only after signatures are registered; coalition could quietly collect 7 signatures before public awareness.
failure_mode: Stealth amendment; capture of 7 keyholders before any public challenge period.
recommended_fix_shape: Add requirement: proposal must be published to public amendment log and Federated Ombuds notified at least 30 days before keyholder signing may begin.

summary: CIP cannot be constituted until P-014 §5 post-activation audit is complete; P-014 has not been executed (P-013 is PROPOSED); Tier 1 amendments are suspended indefinitely until founding sequence completes.
failure_mode: Governing coalition delays P-014 execution to block all Tier 1 amendments.
recommended_fix_shape: Document this dependency chain explicitly; determine whether there should be an interim Tier 1 review mechanism before CIP is constituted. HUMAN JUDGMENT REQUIRED.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: High — any interim mechanism creates a bypass path.

summary: 'Human dignity floor' is undefined beyond the CSM physical basket; non-physical dignity violations (discrimination, coercion, surveillance) are outside the floor's enforceable scope.
failure_mode: Jurisdiction excludes non-physical dignity protections by arguing 'dignity floor' = CSM basket.
recommended_fix_shape: Annotate INVARIANTS.md with a definitional extension of INV-001 naming non-physical dignity components.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: Medium — expanding dignity definition expands the corpus's scope of obligation; human judgment required.

summary: Three incompatible emergency trigger standards with different actors; no master definition or unified trigger test.
failure_mode: Governance emergency declared using lowest-bar standard to invoke interim authority that bypasses deliberation.
recommended_fix_shape: Add master emergency taxonomy (Supply / Governance / Infrastructure) with explicit trigger tests to the protected-terms registry.
risk_introduced_by_fixing: Medium — master definition may not cover all legitimate emergency types.

summary: Individual-level informed consent for pilot participation is not binding until P-059 VPCP is ACTIVE; community consent (2/3 vote) is defined but individual consent is not.
failure_mode: Pilot enrollment without binding consent protocol; coercive or uninformed enrollment.
recommended_fix_shape: Activate P-059 before any pilot enrollment; add to priority acceptance order in Acceptance_Protocol.md.

summary: PRD- IDs used in INVARIANTS.md, Patch_Log, and several annexes but no registry document exists in current corpus; origin document not found.
failure_mode: Cross-referencing PRD- IDs fails; traceability gaps in Tier 1 invariant attack-vector documentation.
recommended_fix_shape: Create one-page PRD- cross-reference table mapping each ID to its T- or P- equivalent.

summary: Two parallel threat registers (TR- in ANNEX_B and T- in Threat_Register.md) with no cross-reference between them.
failure_mode: Threat tracking gap; mitigations in one register unaware of complementary controls in the other.
recommended_fix_shape: Add cross-reference header in ANNEX_B pointing to Threat_Register.md.

summary: The token mechanism absence (RC-001), FC-YT1/FC-YT2 registration gap (RC-002), and Ombuds constitution gap (RC-003) collectively mean INV-LAUNCH-1 cannot be cleared; the launch gate is blocked by three simultaneous conditions.
failure_mode: System cannot proceed to operational phase with real persons until all three conditions are met in sequence.
recommended_fix_shape: Resolve P3-RC-001, P3-RC-002, P3-RC-003 in sequence before any real-persons enrollment; explicitly document all three as pre-launch blocking gates.
